The java files for this game are each in seperate java files and some parts are not entirely organized so this is just a collection of the java files
because I made them seperately for each operation:


public class MeanDeviation {

	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		double[] arr1 = new double[100];
		for (int i = 0; i < arr1.length; i++) {
			double random_number = Math.random() * 100;
			arr1[i] = random_number;
			random_number = (int)(random_number * 100) / 100.0;
			arr1[i] = random_number;
		}	
		
		int index = 0;
		for (double e: arr1) {
			index++;
			System.out.print(e + "  ");
			if (index % 10 == 0) {
				System.out.println(e);
			}
		}
		
		System.out.println("\nMinimum: " + find_min(arr1));
		System.out.println("Maximum: " + find_max(arr1));
		System.out.println("Mean: " + find_mean(arr1));
		System.out.println("Standard Deviation: " + find_standard_deviation(arr1));
	}
	
	public static double find_max(double[] a) {
		double max = a[0];
		for (double e: a) {
			if (e > max) {
				max = e;
			}
		}
		return max;
	}

	public static double find_min(double[] a) {
		double min = a[0];
		for (double e: a) {
			if (e < min) {
				min = e;
			}
		}
		return min;
	}
	
	public static double find_mean(double[] a) {
		double sum = 0.0;
		for (double e: a) {
			sum += e;
		}
		double mean = sum / a.length;
		return (int)(mean * 100) / 100.0;
	}
	
	public static double find_standard_deviation(double[] a) {
		double sum = find_mean(a);
		double standard_deviation = 0.0;
		for (double e: a) {
			standard_deviation += (e - sum) * (e - sum);
		}
		standard_deviation = Math.sqrt(standard_deviation / a.length - 1);
		return (int)(standard_deviation * 100) / 100.0;
	}
}


import java.util.*;

public class Distinct {

	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		Scanner input = new Scanner(System.in);
		System.out.print("What is the length of the array? ");
		int size = input.nextInt();
		int[] arr = new int[size];
		
		int count = 0;
		System.out.print("Enter " + size + " numbers: ");
		for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
			int num = input.nextInt();
			if (!already_there(num, arr)) {
				arr[count] = num;
				count++;
			}
		}
		
		System.out.println("Number of distinct numbers: " + count);
		System.out.print("The distinct Numbers: ");
		for(int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
			System.out.print(arr[i] + " ");
		}
	}
	
	public static boolean already_there(int n, int[] a) {
		for (int e: a) {
			if (e == n) {return true;}
		}
		return false;
	}
}


public class AnalyzeNumbers {

	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		double[] array = {3.4, 5, 6, 1, 6.5, 7.8, 3.5, 9.8, 4, 2.1};
		double sum = 0;
		
		for (double e: array) {
			sum += e;
		}
		
		double average = sum / array.length;
		System.out.println("average is " + average);
	}

}



public class PassByValue {

	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		int size = 5;
		int[] arr = new int[size];
		for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
			arr[i] = i + 1;
		}
		
		// Before
		for (int e: arr) {
			System.out.print(e + " ");
		}
		System.out.println("size is " + size);
		
		// During
		change_array(size, arr);
		
		// After
		for (int e: arr) {
			System.out.print(e + " ");
		}
		System.out.println("size is " + size);
		
		System.out.println("Size reverts back but the array does not");
	}
	
	public static void change_array(int s, int[] a) {
		s = 10;
		a[0] = 67;
		for (int e: a) {
			System.out.print(e + " ");
		}
		System.out.println("size is " + s);
	}

}
